## Agent's Thoughts (2026-04-08 23:14:11)

The user is describing an issue with a chat session feature. Let me break down what they're experiencing:

1. They send a message in a chat session
2. When they refresh the website
3. The input box is disabled/waiting
4. The last "Agent" message box disappears
5. After waiting, it reappears
6. They can see "thought_chuck" coming to the browser

This sounds like a real-time chat application where:
- There's an input for sending messages
- There's a display area showing agent responses
- There might be some streaming or real-time communication happening
- The "thought_chuck" (probably "thought_chunk") is a streaming response piece

The issue seems to be that when the page loads after a refresh, the UI is in a "waiting" state because:
- The last agent message isn't fully loaded/visible
- The system thinks there's still a pending response

Let me explore the codebase to understand the architecture and find where this issue might be occurring.

First, let me check what's in the openspec system and then explore the chat-related code.

41:   // Connect to chat WebSocket
42:   function connectWebSocket(sessionId) {
43:     const wsUrl = `${window.location.protocol === 'https:' ? 'wss:' : 'ws:'}//${window.location.host}/ws/chat/${sessionId}`;
44:     
45:     chatSocket = new WebSocket(wsUrl);
46:     
47:     chatSocket.onopen = () => {
48:       console.log('Chat WebSocket connected');
49:       showConnectionStatus('connected');
50:       
51:       // Request current state from agent

53:         type: 'request_state',
54:         sessionId: currentSessionId,
55:       }));
56:     };
57:     
58:     chatSocket.onmessage = (event) => {
59:       const data = JSON.parse(event.data);
60:       handleWebSocketMessage(data);
61:     };
62:     
63:     chatSocket.onclose = () => {
64:       console.log('Chat WebSocket disconnected');
65:       showConnectionStatus('disconnected');
66:       
67:       // Attempt reconnect after 3 seconds
68:       setTimeout(() => {
69:         if (currentSessionId) {
70:           connectWebSocket(currentSessionId);
71:         }
72:       }, 3000);
73:     };
74:     
75:     chatSocket.onerror = (error) => {
76:       console.error('Chat WebSocket error:', error);
77:       showConnectionStatus('error');
78:     };
79:   }

81:   // Handle incoming WebSocket messages
82:   function handleWebSocketMessage(data) {
83:     console.log('[CHAT] Received message type:', data.type, data);
84:     
85:     switch (data.type) {
86:       case 'prompt_received':
87:         createWaitingAgentMessage();
88:         break;
89: 
90:       case 'thought_start':
91:         startThoughtSection();
92:         break;
93:         
94:       case 'thought_chunk':
95:         appendThoughtChunk(data.content);
96:         break;
97:         
98:       case 'thought_end':
99:         endThoughtSection();
100:         break;
101:         
102:       case 'message_chunk':
103:         appendMessageChunk(data.content);
104:         break;
105:         
106:       case 'usage_update':
107:         updateUsageDisplay(data.usage);
108:         endMessageStream();
109:         break;
110:         
111:       case 'message':
112:         // Check if this is a user message we already added locally
113:         if (data.role === 'user' && pendingUserMessages.has(data.content)) {
114:           pendingUserMessages.delete(data.content);
115:           return; // Skip - already added locally
116:         }
117:         addMessageToChat(data);
118:         break;
119:         
120:       case 'error':
121:         // Clean up any waiting/streaming element before showing the error
122:         if (currentMessageElement) {
123:           currentMessageElement.remove();
124:           currentMessageElement = null;
125:           currentMessageContent = null;
126:           currentThoughtElement = null;
127:           currentThoughtContent = null;
128:         }
129:         showError(data.message);
130:         break;
131:         
132:       case 'history':
133:         loadChatHistory(data.messages);
134:         break;
135: 
136:       case 'session_initialized':
137:         // Initialize model and mode selectors
138:         console.log('[INIT] session_initialized received:', data);
139:         if (data.modelState) {
140:           updateModelSelector(data.modelState);
141:         } else {
142:           console.log('[INIT] No modelState in message');
143:         }
144:         if (data.modeState) {
145:           updateModeSelector(data.modeState);
146:         } else {
147:           console.log('[INIT] No modeState in message');
148:         }
149:         break;
150: 
151:       case 'model_state':
152:         // Update model selector
153:         if (data.modelState) {
154:           updateModelSelector(data.modelState);
155:         }
156:         break;
157: 
158:       case 'mode_state':
159:         // Update mode selector
160:         if (data.modeState) {
161:           updateModeSelector(data.modeState);
162:         }
163:         break;
164: 
165:       case 'permission_request':
166:         showPermissionCard(data);
167:         break;
168: 
169:       case 'permission_resolved':
170:         removePermissionCard(data.requestId);
171:         break;
172:     }
173:   }

175:   function showPermissionCard(data) {
176:     const { requestId, toolCall, options } = data;
177:     const chatMessages = document.querySelector('#chat-messages');
178:     if (!chatMessages) return;
179: 
180:     const card = document.createElement('div');
181:     card.className = 'permission-card';
182:     card.dataset.requestId = requestId;
183: 
184:     const kindLabel = toolCall?.kind ? `<span class="permission-kind">${toolCall.kind}</span>` : '';
185:     const title = toolCall?.title || 'Tool action';
186:     const locations = (toolCall?.locations || [])
187:       .map(loc => `<li>${loc.path}${loc.startLine != null ? `:${loc.startLine}` : ''}</li>`)
188:       .join('');
189:     const locationsList = locations ? `<ul class="permission-locations">${locations}</ul>` : '';
190: 
191:     const buttons = (options || []).map(opt =>
192:       `<button class="permission-btn permission-btn--${opt.kind}" data-option-id="${opt.optionId}">${opt.name}</button>`
193:     ).join('');
194: 
195:     card.innerHTML = `
196:       <div class="permission-card__header">
197:         <span class="permission-card__title">${title}</span>
198:         ${kindLabel}
199:       </div>
200:       ${locationsList}
201:       <div class="permission-card__actions">${buttons}</div>
202:     `;
203: 
204:     card.querySelectorAll('.permission-btn').forEach(btn => {
205:       btn.addEventListener('click', () => {
206:         const optionId = btn.dataset.optionId;
207:         if (chatSocket && chatSocket.readyState === WebSocket.OPEN) {
208:           chatSocket.send(JSON.stringify({ type: 'permission_response', requestId, optionId }));
209:         }
210:         removePermissionCard(requestId);
211:       });
212:     });
213: 
214:     chatMessages.appendChild(card);
215:     scrollToBottom();
216:   }

218:   function removePermissionCard(requestId) {

223:   // Create the editable YOU bubble at the bottom of chat-messages

258:     // Editable content area

262:     content.setAttribute('data-placeholder', 'Type a message...');
263: 
264:     // Ctrl+Enter to send; Enter inserts newline (native contenteditable behavior)

288:     // Apply current connection status to the new bubble

292:   // Send the editable bubble's content
293:   function submitEditableBubble() {
294:     if (!editableBubble) return;
295: 
296:     const content = editableBubble.querySelector('.message-content[contenteditable]');
297:     if (!content) return;
298: 
299:     const message = content.innerText.trim();
300:     if (!message) return;
301: 
302:     // Convert bubble to read-only: remove contenteditable and header controls
303:     const header = editableBubble.querySelector('.editable-bubble-header');
304:     if (header) header.remove();
305:     content.removeAttribute('contenteditable');
306:     editableBubble.classList.remove('editable-bubble');
307: 
308:     // Replace with a static message-header
309:     const staticHeader = document.createElement('div');
310:     staticHeader.className = 'message-header';
311:     staticHeader.textContent = 'You';
312:     editableBubble.insertBefore(staticHeader, content);
313: 
314:     editableBubble = null;
315: 
316:     // Add to pending to avoid duplicate when server echoes
317:     pendingUserMessages.add(message);
318: 
319:     // Send via WebSocket or HTTP fallback
320:     if (chatSocket && chatSocket.readyState === WebSocket.OPEN) {
321:       chatSocket.send(JSON.stringify({
322:         type: 'send_message',
323:         content: message,
324:       }));
325:     } else {
326:       sendMessageHttp(message);
327:     }
328:   }

330:   // Send message via HTTP fallback
331:   async function sendMessageHttp(message) {
332:     try {
333:       const res = await fetch(`/sessions/${currentSessionId}/chat`, {
334:         method: 'POST',
335:         headers: {
336:           'Content-Type': 'application/x-www-form-urlencoded',
337:         },
338:         body: new URLSearchParams({ message }).toString(),
339:       });
340:       
341:       if (!res.ok) {
342:         showError('Failed to send message');
343:       }
344:     } catch (error) {
345:       showError('Failed to send message: ' + error.message);
346:     }
347:   }

479:     // Wait for message element to exist

677:   // Update usage display
678:   function updateUsageDisplay(usage) {
679:     const usageEl = document.querySelector('#chat-usage');
680:     if (!usageEl) return;
681:     
682:     if (!usage) {
683:       usageEl.textContent = '';
684:       usageEl.style.display = 'none';
685:       return;
686:     }
687:     
688:     const parts = [];
689:     
690:     // Show cost if available
691:     if (usage.cost?.amount !== undefined) {
692:       const amount = usage.cost.amount / 100;
693:       parts.push(`Cost: $${amount.toFixed(4)}`);
694:     }
695:     
696:     // Show tokens used if available
697:     if (usage.used !== undefined) {
698:       parts.push(`Tokens: ${usage.used.toLocaleString()}`);
699:     }
700:     
701:     // Show context size if available
702:     if (usage.size !== undefined) {
703:       parts.push(`Context: ${usage.size.toLocaleString()}`);
704:     }
705:     
706:     if (parts.length > 0) {
707:       usageEl.textContent = parts.join(' | ');
708:       usageEl.style.display = 'block';
709:     } else {
710:       usageEl.textContent = '';
711:       usageEl.style.display = 'none';
712:     }
713:   }
714:

732:     statusEl.style.color = status === 'connected' ? '#51cf66' : '#888';

753:   // Load chat history
754:   function loadChatHistory(messages) {
755:     const chatContainer = document.querySelector('#chat-messages');
756:     if (!chatContainer) return;
757: 
758:     // Clear existing messages and any existing editable bubble
759:     chatContainer.innerHTML = '';
760:     editableBubble = null;
761:     
762:     // Track current thought and message for grouping
763:     let currentThoughtText = '';
764:     let currentMessageText = '';
765:     let inThought = false;
766:     let inMessage = false;
767:     let lastRole = null;
768: 
769:     messages.forEach(msg => {
770:       // Skip available_commands_update in history
771:       if (msg.content && msg.content.includes('available_commands_update')) {
772:         return;
773:       }
774:       
775:       // Try to parse JSON content for assistant messages
776:       if (msg.role === 'assistant' && msg.content) {
777:         try {
778:           const parsed = JSON.parse(msg.content);
779:           if (parsed.update) {
780:             const updateType = parsed.update.sessionUpdate;
781:             const text = parsed.update.content?.text || '';
782:             
783:             // Handle thought chunks
784:             if (updateType === 'agent_thought_chunk') {
785:               if (!inThought) {
786:                 inThought = true;
787:                 currentThoughtText = '';
788:               }
789:               currentThoughtText += text;
790:               return; // Skip adding as individual message
791:             }
792:             
793:             // Handle message chunks
794:             if (updateType === 'agent_message_chunk') {
795:               if (inThought) {
796:                 // End thought section first
797:                 addThoughtToChat(chatContainer, currentThoughtText);
798:                 inThought = false;
799:                 currentThoughtText = '';
800:               }
801:               if (!inMessage) {
802:                 inMessage = true;
803:                 currentMessageText = '';
804:               }
805:               currentMessageText += text;
806:               return; // Skip adding as individual message
807:             }
808:             
809:             // Handle usage
810:             if (updateType === 'usage_update') {
811:               updateUsageDisplay(parsed.update.cost || {});
812:               return;
813:             }
814:             
815:             // Skip other types
816:             return;
817:           }
818:         } catch (e) {
819:           // Not JSON, display as-is
820:         }
821:       }
822:       
823:       // User message or unparseable content
824:       lastRole = msg.role;
825:       addMessageToChat(msg);
826:     });
827:     
828:     // Add any pending thought
829:     if (inThought && currentThoughtText) {
830:       addThoughtToChat(chatContainer, currentThoughtText);
831:     }
832:     
833:     // Add any pending message
834:     if (inMessage && currentMessageText) {
835:       lastRole = 'assistant';
836:       addMessageToChat({
837:         role: 'assistant',
838:         content: currentMessageText,
839:       });
840:     }
841: 
842:     scrollToBottom();
843: 
844:     // Show editable bubble only if the last message is NOT from the user
845:     // (if last is user, the agent hasn't responded yet — wait for usage_update)
846:     if (lastRole !== 'user') {
847:       createEditableBubble();
848:     }
849:   }

23:   private findSessionDir(sessionId: string): string | null {
24:     const Paths = getPaths();
25:     if (!existsSync(Paths.projects)) {
26:       return null;
27:     }
28: 
29:     const { readdirSync } = require("fs");
30:     const projectEntries = readdirSync(Paths.projects, { withFileTypes: true });
31:     
32:     for (const projectEntry of projectEntries) {
33:       if (projectEntry.isDirectory()) {
34:         const sessionsDir = join(Paths.projects, projectEntry.name, "sessions");
35:         if (existsSync(sessionsDir)) {
36:           const sessionDir = join(sessionsDir, sessionId);
37:           if (existsSync(sessionDir)) {
38:             return sessionDir;
39:           }
40:         }
41:       }
42:     }
43: 
44:     return null;
45:   }

47:   async saveMessage(sessionId: string, message: ChatMessage): Promise<void> {
48:     const chatPath = this.getChatPath(sessionId);
49:     const line = JSON.stringify(message) + "\n";
50:     appendFileSync(chatPath, line, "utf-8");

52:

57:         return [];

## Agent's Thoughts (2026-04-08 23:14:23)

Now I understand the architecture better. Let me trace what happens:

1. Frontend: `chat.js` manages the WebSocket connection and UI
2. Backend: `chat.ts` is the ChatService that saves/loads chat history

The issue the user describes:
1. Send a message
2. Refresh the website
3. Input box is disabled (waiting state)
4. The last "Agent" box disappears
5. After waiting, it reappears
6. They can see "thought_chunk" coming to the browser

The key insight is in `loadChatHistory` (chat.js lines 754-849):

```javascript
// Show editable bubble only if the last message is NOT from the user
// (if last is user, the agent hasn't responded yet — wait for usage_update)
if (lastRole !== 'user') {
  createEditableBubble();
}
```

And the condition for `lastRole`:
```javascript
// User message or unparseable content
lastRole = msg.role;
addMessageToChat(msg);
```

So the logic is:
- If the last message is from the user → don't show the editable bubble (agent is still processing)
- If the last message is from the assistant → show the editable bubble

Now, the problem:

When refreshing, the WebSocket reconnects and sends `request_state`:
```javascript
chatSocket.onopen = () => {
  console.log('Chat WebSocket connected');
  showConnectionStatus('connected');
  
  // Request current state from agent
  chatSocket.send(JSON.stringify({
    type: 'request_state',
    sessionId: currentSessionId,
  }));
};
```

The server probably sends back the chat history. The `loadChatHistory` function processes the history.

The issue is: the history might be showing messages in a streaming/incomplete state. 

Looking at the history loading logic, it processes messages in order and only sets `lastRole` when encountering user messages or content that can't be parsed. If the last assistant message contains streamed content, `lastRole` might not get updated properly, causing the editable bubble to not appear even though the agent has finished responding.

I need to trace through what happens when `request_state` is sent and how the server responds. The `request_state` handler on the server side would determine what history gets sent back to the client, and if the message format differs from what's expected, the logic for determining whether the bubble should be shown could break down. Looking at the message handling flow, there's a guard that checks for a `currentMessageElement` before creating the streaming agent message, but if that element was already cleared or never properly initialized during history reconstruction, the streaming indicator won't be created. Then when `thought_chunk` messages arrive, `appendThoughtChunk` attempts to call `startThoughtSection()` if the thought content is missing, but this might fail if the message structure isn't properly set up from the previous state.

The issue seems to be in `loadChatHistory` - it clears the chat container and rebuilds it, which could remove the streaming element if one existed when the refresh happened. The flow should be: page loads and requests current state, server responds with history and ongoing state, history reconstructs previous messages, but if there was an active streaming session, the state might not properly restore that streaming element before the next chunk arrives. if the last message is from the user, the editable bubble isn't created and the user appears stuck waiting. The real problem emerges when refreshing mid-stream—the server sends a partial `prompt_received` message in the history, which loads as an incomplete user message without a corresponding agent response, triggering this blocking behavior. I need to examine how the server handles the `request_state` message to see what state information it actually sends back.

21: const router = new Hono();

23: // Helper to get authenticated username from cookie
24: async function getAuthUsername(c: Context): Promise<string | null> {
25:   const cookieHeader = c.req.header("Cookie");
26:   const usernameMatch = cookieHeader?.match(/username=([^;]+)/);
27:   const username = usernameMatch ? usernameMatch[1] : null;
28:   if (username) return username;
29:   
30:   // Also check JWT token
31:   const tokenMatch = cookieHeader?.match(/token=([^;]+)/);
32:   const token = tokenMatch ? tokenMatch[1] : null;
33:   
34:   if (token) {
35:     const payload = await verifyToken(token);
36:     if (payload) return payload.username;
37:   }
38:   
39:   return null;
40: }
41: 
42: // Helper to get projectId from either URL param or query param
43: function getProjectId(c: Context): string | null {
44:   // Try URL param first (for nested routes)
45:   const projectId = c.req.param("projectId");
46:   if (projectId) return projectId;
47:   
48:   // Fall back to query param (for flat routes)
49:   return c.req.query("projectId");
50: }
51: 
52: // GET /sessions or /projects/:projectId/sessions - List sessions for a project
53: router.get("/", async (c: Context) => {
54:   const username = await getAuthUsername(c);
55:   if (!username) {
56:     return c.redirect("/auth/login");
57:   }
58: 
59:   const projectId = getProjectId(c);
60:   if (!projectId) {
61:     return c.text("Project ID required", 400);
62:   }
63: 
64:   const project = await projectRepository.findById(projectId);
65:   if (!project || project.owner !== username) {
66:     return c.text("Project not found", 404);
67:   }
68: 
69:   const sessions = await sessionRepository.listByProject(projectId);
70: 
71:   return c.html(
72:     <SessionListPage project={project} sessions={sessions} />
73:   );
74: });
75: 
76: // GET /sessions/new or /projects/:projectId/sessions/new - Create session form
77: router.get("/new", async (c: Context) => {
78:   const username = await getAuthUsername(c);
79:   if (!username) {
80:     return c.redirect("/auth/login");
81:   }
82: 
83:   const projectId = getProjectId(c);
84:   if (!projectId) {
85:     return c.text("Project ID required", 400);
86:   }
87: 
88:   const project = await projectRepository.findById(projectId);
89:   if (!project || project.owner !== username) {
90:     return c.text("Project not found", 404);
91:   }
92: 
93:   const agents = await agentService.listAgentsByOwner(username);
94: 
95:   return c.html(
96:     <SessionCreatePage project={project} agents={agents} />
97:   );
98: });
99: 
100: // POST /sessions or /projects/:projectId/sessions - Create new session
101: router.post("/", async (c: Context) => {
102:   const username = await getAuthUsername(c);
103:   if (!username) {
104:     return c.redirect("/auth/login");
105:   }
106: 
107:   const body = await c.req.parseBody();
108:   const name = body.name as string;
109:   const projectId = (body.projectId as string) || getProjectId(c);
110:   const assignedAgentId = (body.assignedAgentId as string) || null;
111:   const localDevMirrorPath = (body.localDevMirrorPath as string) || null;
112:   const agentSubpath = (body.agentSubpath as string) || null;
113: 
114:   if (!name || !projectId) {
115:     return c.text("Name and project ID required", 400);
116:   }
117: 
118:   const project = await projectRepository.findById(projectId);
119:   if (!project || project.owner !== username) {
120:     return c.text("Project not found", 404);
121:   }
122: 
123:   // Create session with upstream and checkout directories
124:   const session = await sessionRepository.create({
125:     name: name as string,
126:     projectId: projectId as string,
127:     owner: username,
128:     assignedAgentId: assignedAgentId || undefined,
129:     localDevMirrorPath: localDevMirrorPath || undefined,
130:     agentSubpath: agentSubpath || undefined,
131:   });
132: 
133:   // Initialize repository: clone → import to fossil
134:   // Note: checkout is created by agent when it receives session_ready
135:   try {
136:     // Step 1: Clone repository to upstream/
137:     const cloneResult = await vcs.cloneRepository(
138:       project.repoUrl,
139:       project.repoType,
140:       session.upstreamPath,
141:       undefined,
142:       project.sourceBranch
143:     );
144:     
145:     if (!cloneResult.success) {
146:       await sessionRepository.delete(projectId, session.id);
147:       return c.text(`Failed to clone repository: ${cloneResult.error}`, 500);
148:     }
149: 
150:     // Step 2: Import to fossil proxy (repo.fossil)
151:     const fossilPath = `${session.upstreamPath}/../repo.fossil`;
152:     const importResult = await vcs.importToFossil(
153:       session.upstreamPath,
154:       project.repoType,
155:       fossilPath
156:     );
157:     
158:     if (!importResult.success) {
159:       await sessionRepository.delete(projectId, session.id);
160:       return c.text(`Failed to import to fossil: ${importResult.error}`, 500);
161:     }
162: 
163:     // Step 3: Create new branch if specified (after Fossil import is complete)
164:     if (project.newBranch) {
165:       const branchResult = await vcs.createBranch(
166:         project.newBranch,
167:         project.repoType,
168:         session.upstreamPath
169:       );
170:       if (!branchResult.success) {
171:         await sessionRepository.delete(projectId, session.id);
172:         return c.text(`Failed to create branch '${project.newBranch}': ${branchResult.error}`, 500);
173:       }
174:     }
175: 
176:     // Step 4: Create fossil user for agent access
177:     const agentWorkspaceUser = `agent-${session.id.slice(0, 8)}`;
178:     const agentWorkspacePassword = crypto.randomUUID().replace(/-/g, "").slice(0, 16);
179:     const userResult = await vcs.createFossilUser(fossilPath, agentWorkspaceUser, agentWorkspacePassword);
180:     
181:     if (!userResult.success) {
182:       console.error("[session] Failed to create fossil user:", userResult.error);
183:       // Non-fatal - session can still work, just log the error
184:     }
185:     
186:     // Save credentials to session
187:     await sessionRepository.update(session.id, {
188:       agentWorkspaceUser,
189:       agentWorkspacePassword,
190:     });
191:     
192:     // Step 5: Open fossil checkout in agent-workspace
193:     const openResult = await vcs.openFossil(fossilPath, session.agentWorkspacePath);
194:     if (!openResult.success) {
195:       console.error("[session] Failed to open fossil in agent-workspace:", openResult.error);
196:       await sessionRepository.delete(projectId, session.id);
197:       return c.text("Failed to open fossil checkout", 500);
198:     }
199: 
200:     // Step 6: Notify running agent if one is assigned and online
201:     if (assignedAgentId && agentService.isAgentOnline(assignedAgentId)) {
202:       const agentWs = agentService.getAgentConnection(assignedAgentId);
203:       if (agentWs && agentWs.readyState === 1) {
204:         const serverResult = await fossilServerManager.startServer(session.id, fossilPath);
205:         if ('port' in serverResult) {
206:           await sessionRepository.update(session.id, { port: serverResult.port });
207:           const sessionWithCreds = await sessionRepository.findById(session.id);
208:           const platformUrl = process.env.PLATFORM_URL ?? `http://localhost:${process.env.PORT ?? 3000}`;
209:           agentWs.send(JSON.stringify({
210:             type: 'session_ready',
211:             platformUrl,
212:             sessions: [{
213:               sessionId: session.id,
214:               name: session.name,
215:               upstreamPath: session.upstreamPath,
216:               agentWorkspacePath: session.agentWorkspacePath,
217:               port: serverResult.port,
218:               agentWorkspaceUser: sessionWithCreds?.agentWorkspaceUser,
219:               agentWorkspacePassword: sessionWithCreds?.agentWorkspacePassword,
220:               acpSessionId: sessionWithCreds?.acpSessionId ?? null,
221:               localDevMirrorPath: sessionWithCreds?.localDevMirrorPath ?? null,
222:               agentSubpath: sessionWithCreds?.agentSubpath ?? null,
223:             }],
224:           }));
225:           console.log(`[session] Notified running agent ${assignedAgentId} of new session ${session.id}`);
226:         }
227:       }
228:     }
229:   } catch (error) {
230:     console.error("Failed to setup session:", error);
231:     await sessionRepository.delete(projectId, session.id);
232:     return c.text("Failed to setup session repository", 500);
233:   }
234: 
235:   return c.redirect(`/projects/${projectId}/sessions/${session.id}`);
236: });
237:

242:     return c.redirect("/auth/login");

327: // GET /sessions/:id/files - Get file tree for a session
328: router.get("/:id/files", async (c: Context) => {
329:   const username = await getAuthUsername(c);
330:   if (!username) {
331:     return c.redirect("/auth/login");
332:   }
333: 
334:   const sessionId = c.req.param("id");
335:   const session = await sessionRepository.findById(sessionId);
336:   
337:   if (!session || session.owner !== username) {
338:     return c.text("Session not found", 404);
339:   }
340: 
341:   // Scan filesystem directly for files
342:   const { readdirSync, statSync } = await import("fs");
343:   const { join, relative } = await import("path");
344:   
345:   const fileTree: { [key: string]: string[] } = {};
346:   
347:   function scanDir(dirPath: string, basePath: string) {
348:     if (!statSync(dirPath, { throwIfNoEntry: false })) {
349:       return;
350:     }
351:     
352:     const entries = readdirSync(dirPath, { withFileTypes: true });
353:     
354:     for (const entry of entries) {
355:       const fullPath = join(dirPath, entry.name);
356:       const relPath = relative(basePath, fullPath);
357:       
358:       if (entry.isDirectory()) {
359:         scanDir(fullPath, basePath);
360:       } else {
361:         const parts = relPath.split("/");
362:         const fileName = parts.pop() || "";
363:         const dir = parts.length > 0 ? parts.join("/") : "(root)";
364:         
365:         if (!fileTree[dir]) {
366:           fileTree[dir] = [];
367:         }
368:         fileTree[dir].push(fileName);
369:       }
370:     }
371:   }
372:   
373:   try {
374:     scanDir(session.agentWorkspacePath, session.agentWorkspacePath);
375:   } catch (error) {
376:     console.error("Error scanning checkout:", error);
377:   }
378: 
379:   // Return HTML file tree
380:   let html = `<!DOCTYPE html>
381: <html>
382: <head><title>File Tree</title></head>
383: <body>
384: <div class="file-tree">
385: `;
386: 
387:   for (const [dir, files] of Object.entries(fileTree)) {
388:     html += `  <div class="dir">${dir}/</div>\n`;
389:     for (const file of files) {
390:       html += `    <div class="file">${file}</div>\n`;
391:     }
392:   }
393: 
394:   html += `</div>
395: </body>
396: </html>`;
397: 
398:   return c.html(html);
399: });

401: // POST /sessions/:id/chat - Save a chat message

431: // GET /sessions/:id/impact - Get real-time impact metrics for a session
432: router.get("/:id/impact", async (c: Context) => {
433:   const username = await getAuthUsername(c);
434:   if (!username) {
435:     return c.json({ error: "Unauthorized" }, 401);
436:   }
437: 
438:   const sessionId = c.req.param("id");
439:   const session = await sessionRepository.findById(sessionId);
440: 
441:   if (!session || session.owner !== username) {
442:     return c.json({ error: "Session not found" }, 404);
443:   }
444: 
445:   try {
446:     const { impactCalculator } = await import("../impact/calculator.js");
447:     const { getSccService } = await import("../impact/scc-service.js");
448:     const { vcs } = await import("../vcs/index.js");
449: 
450:     // Sync agent-workspace with repo.fossil before calculating impact
451:     const fossilPath = `${session.upstreamPath}/../repo.fossil`;
452:     const { existsSync } = await import("fs");
453:     const { join } = await import("path");
454:     const fslckoutPath = join(session.agentWorkspacePath, ".fslckout");
455:     
456:     if (existsSync(fossilPath)) {
457:       if (!existsSync(fslckoutPath)) {
458:         // Initialize fossil checkout if not exists
459:         console.log(`[impact] Initializing fossil checkout in agent-workspace...`);
460:         await vcs.openFossil(fossilPath, session.agentWorkspacePath);
461:       }
462:       // Sync with repo.fossil to get latest changes from agent
463:       console.log(`[impact] Syncing agent-workspace with repo.fossil...`);
464:       await vcs.fossilUp(session.agentWorkspacePath);
465:     }
466: 
467:     // Check if scc is installed - use getter to avoid TDZ issues
468:     const sccService = getSccService();
469:     const sccInstalled = sccService.isInstalled();
470: 
471:     if (!sccInstalled) {
472:       // Return basic file counts without complexity
473:       const { readdirSync, statSync, readFileSync } = await import("fs");
474:       const { join, relative } = await import("path");
475:       const crypto = await import("crypto");
476: 
477:       const scanDir = (dir: string, baseDir: string, files: Map<string, { checksum: string; size: number }>) => {
478:         if (!statSync(dir, { throwIfNoEntry: false })) return;
479:         const entries = readdirSync(dir, { withFileTypes: true });
480:         for (const entry of entries) {
481:           const fullPath = join(dir, entry.name);
482:           const relPath = relative(baseDir, fullPath);
483:           if (entry.name.startsWith(".")) continue;
484:           if (entry.isDirectory()) {
485:             scanDir(fullPath, baseDir, files);
486:           } else {
487:             const stats = statSync(fullPath);
488:             const content = readFileSync(fullPath);
489:             const checksum = crypto.createHash("md5").update(content).digest("hex");
490:             files.set(relPath, { checksum, size: stats.size });
491:           }
492:         }
493:       };
494: 
495:       const upstreamFiles = new Map<string, { checksum: string; size: number }>();
496:       const workspaceFiles = new Map<string, { checksum: string; size: number }>();
497: 
498:       scanDir(session.upstreamPath, session.upstreamPath, upstreamFiles);
499:       scanDir(session.agentWorkspacePath, session.agentWorkspacePath, workspaceFiles);
500: 
501:       let newCount = 0, changedCount = 0, deletedCount = 0;
502: 
503:       for (const [path, info] of workspaceFiles) {
504:         if (!upstreamFiles.has(path)) newCount++;
505:         else if (upstreamFiles.get(path)?.checksum !== info.checksum) changedCount++;
506:       }
507: 
508:       for (const [path] of upstreamFiles) {
509:         if (!workspaceFiles.has(path)) deletedCount++;
510:       }
511: 
512:       return c.json({
513:         files: { new: newCount, changed: changedCount, deleted: deletedCount },
514:         sccInstalled: false,
515:         warning: "scc not installed - complexity metrics unavailable",
516:       });
517:     }
518: 
519:     // Calculate full impact with complexity
520:     const result = await impactCalculator.calculateImpact(
521:       sessionId,
522:       session.upstreamPath,
523:       session.agentWorkspacePath
524:     );
525: 
526:     return c.json({
527:       ...result,
528:       sccInstalled: true,
529:     });
530:   } catch (error) {

538:   const username = await getAuthUsername(c);

540:     return c.json({ error: "Unauthorized" }, 401);

542:

545: 
546:   if (!session || session.owner !== username) {

550:   // Check running server first, then fall back to saved session port

556:       running: true,

105: const server = Bun.serve({
106:   async fetch(req: Request, server: any) {

108:     
109:     // Handle WebSocket upgrade requests
110:     if (req.headers.get("upgrade") === "websocket") {
111:       const type = url.pathname.split('/')[2]; // /ws/agent or /ws/chat
112:       
113:       if (type === 'agent') {
114:         const token = url.searchParams.get("token");

118:         }
119:         
120:         const payload = await agentService.verifyAgentToken(token);

122:           console.log("[WS] Invalid token");

125:         
126:         console.log("[WS] Token verified, agentId:", payload.agentId);
127:

129:           data: {

132:             url: req.url
133:           }
134:         });
135:         
136:         if (!upgraded) {
137:           console.log("[WS] WebSocket upgrade failed");
138:           return new Response("WebSocket upgrade failed", { status: 500 });
139:         }
140:         console.log("[WS] WebSocket upgraded successfully for agent:", payload.agentId);

143:       
144:       if (type === 'chat') {

157:         
158:         if (!upgraded) {

160:         }
161:         return undefined;
162:       }
163:       
164:       return new Response("Unknown WebSocket endpoint", { status: 404 });
165:     }
166:     
167:     return app.fetch(req);
168:   },
169:   port: PORT,
170:   websocket: {
171:     // WebSocket handler for different connection types
172:     async message(ws, message) {
173:       try {
174:         const data = JSON.parse(message as string);
175:         const connectionType = ws.data?.connectionType;
176:         
177:         switch (connectionType) {
178:           case 'agent':
179:             await handleAgentMessage(ws, data);
180:             break;
181:           case 'chat':
182:             await handleChatMessage(ws, data);
183:             break;
184:           default:
185:             console.log("Unknown connection type");
186:         }
187:       } catch (error) {
188:         console.error("WebSocket message error:", error);
189:       }
190:     },
191:     async open(ws) {
192:       const url = new URL(ws.data.url);
193:       const type = url.pathname.split('/')[2]; // /ws/chat or /ws/agent
194:       
195:       if (type === 'chat') {
196:         // Chat connection
197:         const sessionId = url.pathname.split('/')[3];
198:         ws.data.connectionType = 'chat';
199:         ws.data.sessionId = sessionId;
200:         
201:         // Add to session subscribers
202:         if (!chatSessions.has(sessionId)) {
203:           chatSessions.set(sessionId, new Set());
204:         }
205:         chatSessions.get(sessionId).add(ws);
206:         
207:         // Send chat history

210:           type: 'history',
211:           messages: history,
212:         }));
213:         
214:         console.log(`Chat client connected to session ${sessionId}`);
215:       } else {
216:         // Agent connection
217:         const token = url.searchParams.get("token");
218:         
219:         if (!token) {
220:           ws.close(1008, "Missing token");
221:           return;
222:         }
223: 
224:         const payload = await agentService.verifyAgentToken(token);
225:         if (!payload) {
226:           ws.close(1008, "Invalid token");
227:           return;
228:         }
229: 
230:         ws.data.connectionType = 'agent';
231:         ws.data.agentId = payload.agentId;
232:         ws.data.authenticated = true;  // Mark as authenticated, waiting for agent_ready
233:         
234:         await agentService.handleAgentConnect(payload.agentId, ws);
235:         console.log(`Agent ${payload.agentId} connected, waiting for agent_ready`);
236:       }
237:     },
238:     async close(ws) {
239:       const connectionType = ws.data?.connectionType;
240:       
241:       if (connectionType === 'chat') {
242:         // Remove from session subscribers
243:         const sessionId = ws.data.sessionId;
244:         if (chatSessions.has(sessionId)) {
245:           chatSessions.get(sessionId).delete(ws);
246:           if (chatSessions.get(sessionId).size === 0) {

248: 
249:             // Auto-reject any pending permission requests for this session
250:             for (const [requestId, pending] of pendingPermissions) {
251:               if (pending.sessionId === sessionId) {
252:                 pendingPermissions.delete(requestId);
253:                 if (pending.agentWs.readyState === 1) {
254:                   pending.agentWs.send(JSON.stringify({
255:                     type: "permission_response",
256:                     requestId,
257:                     outcome: { outcome: "cancelled" },
258:                   }));
259:                 }
260:               }
261:             }
262:           }
263:         }
264:         console.log(`Chat client disconnected from session ${sessionId}`);
265:       } else if (connectionType === 'agent') {

268:           await agentService.handleAgentDisconnect(agentId);

276:               // Check if agent reconnected

287:           }
288:         }

295: async function handleAgentMessage(ws, data) {
296:   console.log("[agent] Received message:", data.type, data);
297:   process.stdout?.write?.(""); // Flush stdout
298:   
299:   switch (data.type) {
300:     case "ping":
301:       ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: "pong" }));
302:       break;
303:     case "agent_ready":

305:       process.stdout?.write?.(""); // Flush stdout
306:       
307:       // Store workdir for relative path computation

318:       
319:       if (sessions.length > 0) {

334:             
335:             const result = await fossilServerManager.startServer(sessionId, fossilPath);
336:             console.log("[agent] Fossil server result:", result);
337:             process.stdout?.write?.(""); // Flush stdout

339:             if ('port' in result) {
340:               // Update session with port

342:               
343:               // Get session with credentials

358:             } else {

362:         }

376:         }
377:       } else {
378:         console.log("[agent] No sessions assigned to agent");
379:       }
380:       break;
381:     case "thought_start":
382:       {
383:         const startSessionId = data.sessionId;

542:

630:           if (data.modelState) {

643:       }
644:       break;
645:     case "model_state":
646:       // Update and broadcast model state
647:       if (data.sessionId && data.modelState) {

649:         
650:         const modelSubscribers = chatSessions.get(data.sessionId);
651:         if (modelSubscribers) {

661:           });
662:         }
663:       }
664:       break;

682:         }
683:       }
684:       break;
685:     case "prompt_received":
686:       {
687:         const prSubscribers = chatSessions.get(data.sessionId);
688:         if (prSubscribers) {

691:               client.send(JSON.stringify({
692:                 type: "prompt_received",
693:                 sessionId: data.sessionId,
694:                 timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
695:               }));
696:             }
697:           });
698:         }
699:       }
700:       break;
701:     case "permission_request":
702:       {
703:         const { sessionId: permSessionId, requestId, toolCall, options } = data;
704:         if (!permSessionId || !requestId) break;
705: 
706:         // Store requestId → agentWs so we can route the response back
707:         pendingPermissions.set(requestId, { agentWs: ws, sessionId: permSessionId });
708: 
709:         // Broadcast to all chat clients for this session
710:         const permSubscribers = chatSessions.get(permSessionId);
711:         if (permSubscribers) {
712:           permSubscribers.forEach((client: WebSocket) => {
713:             if (client.readyState === 1) {
714:               client.send(JSON.stringify({
715:                 type: "permission_request",
716:                 requestId,
717:                 toolCall,
718:                 options,
719:                 timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
720:               }));
721:             }
722:           });
723:         }
724:       }
725:       break;
726:     default:
727:       console.log("[agent] Unknown message type:", data.type);
728:   }
729: }
730: 
731: // Handle chat messages
732: async function handleChatMessage(ws, data) {
733:   const sessionId = ws.data.sessionId;
734:   
735:   switch (data.type) {
736:     case "send_message":
737:       // Save user message
738:       await chatService.saveMessage(sessionId, {
739:         role: 'user',
740:         content: data.content,
741:         timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
742:       });
743: 
744:       // Broadcast to all clients in session
745:       const subscribers = chatSessions.get(sessionId);
746:       if (subscribers) {
747:         subscribers.forEach(client => {
748:           if (client.readyState === 1) {
749:             client.send(JSON.stringify({
750:               type: 'message',
751:               role: 'user',
752:               content: data.content,
753:               timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
754:             }));
755:           }
756:         });
757:       }
758: 
759:       // Get session to find assigned agent
760:       const session = await sessionRepository.findById(sessionId);
761:       if (session?.assignedAgentId) {
762:         // Forward to agent if connected
763:         const ws = agentService.getAgentConnection(session.assignedAgentId);
764:         if (ws && ws.readyState === 1) {
765:           ws.send(JSON.stringify({
766:             type: 'user_message',
767:             sessionId: sessionId,
768:             content: data.content,
769:           }));
770:         }
771:       }
772:       break;
773: 
774:     case "set_model":
775:       // Forward model change to agent
776:       const modelSession = await sessionRepository.findById(sessionId);
777:       if (modelSession?.assignedAgentId) {
778:         const agentWs = agentService.getAgentConnection(modelSession.assignedAgentId);
779:         if (agentWs && agentWs.readyState === 1) {
780:           agentWs.send(JSON.stringify({
781:             type: 'set_model',
782:             sessionId: sessionId,
783:             modelId: data.modelId,
784:           }));
785:         }
786:       }
787:       break;
788: 
789:     case "set_mode":
790:       // Forward mode change to agent
791:       const modeSession = await sessionRepository.findById(sessionId);
792:       if (modeSession?.assignedAgentId) {
793:         const modeAgentWs = agentService.getAgentConnection(modeSession.assignedAgentId);
794:         if (modeAgentWs && modeAgentWs.readyState === 1) {
795:           modeAgentWs.send(JSON.stringify({
796:             type: 'set_mode',
797:             sessionId: sessionId,
798:             modeId: data.modeId,
799:           }));
800:         }
801:       }
802:       break;
803: 
804:     case "request_state":
805:       // Forward state request to agent
806:       const stateSession = await sessionRepository.findById(sessionId);
807:       if (stateSession?.assignedAgentId) {
808:         const stateAgentWs = agentService.getAgentConnection(stateSession.assignedAgentId);
809:         if (stateAgentWs && stateAgentWs.readyState === 1) {
810:           stateAgentWs.send(JSON.stringify({
811:             type: 'request_state',
812:             sessionId: sessionId,
813:           }));
814:         }
815:       }
816:       break;
817:       
818:     case "request_replay":
819:       const history = await chatService.loadHistory(sessionId);
820:       ws.send(JSON.stringify({
821:         type: 'history',
822:         messages: history,
823:       }));
824:       break;
825:       
826:     case "permission_response":
827:       {
828:         const { requestId, optionId } = data;
829:         const pending = pendingPermissions.get(requestId);
830:         if (!pending) break;
831: 
832:         pendingPermissions.delete(requestId);
833: 
834:         // Route response back to agent
835:         if (pending.agentWs.readyState === 1) {
836:           pending.agentWs.send(JSON.stringify({
837:             type: "permission_response",
838:             requestId,
839:             outcome: { outcome: "selected", optionId },
840:           }));
841:         }
842: 
843:         // Broadcast resolution to all chat clients so other tabs dismiss the card
844:         const resolveSubscribers = chatSessions.get(pending.sessionId);
845:         if (resolveSubscribers) {
846:           resolveSubscribers.forEach((client: WebSocket) => {
847:             if (client.readyState === 1) {
848:               client.send(JSON.stringify({
849:                 type: "permission_resolved",
850:                 requestId,
851:               }));
852:             }
853:           });
854:         }
855:       }
856:       break;
857: 
858:     default:
859:       console.log("Unknown chat message type:", data.type);
860:   }
861: }
862: 
863: // Server is ready
864: console.log(`Server running at http://localhost:${server.port}`);

(End of file - total 864 lines)
</content>
```

## Agent (2026-04-08 23:16:28)

I found the issue. Let me trace through what happens:

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                         REFRESH TIMELINE                            │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                     │
│  BEFORE REFRESH (browser tab 1):                                    │
│  - User sent message → saved to chat.jsonl                          │
│  - prompt_received → "Received, processing..." indicator            │
│  - Agent streaming → thought_chunk, message_chunk                    │
│                                                                     │
│  ON REFRESH:                                                        │
│  - WebSocket reconnects                                             │
│  - Server sends: { type: 'history', messages: [user_msg] }          │
│                   ↑ Only saved because it's instant                 │
│                                                                     │
│  AFTER REFRESH (new tab):                                           │
│  - loadChatHistory runs                                             │
│  - Shows user message only                                          │
│  - lastRole = 'user' → NO editable bubble                          │
│  - Input is effectively invisible/disabled                          │
│                                                                     │
│  WAITING...                                                         │
│  - prompt_received arrives → "Received, processing..."              │
│  - thought_chunk arrives → "Agent" box appears with thought         │
│  - message_chunk arrives → response fills in                       │
│  - usage_update arrives → editable bubble finally appears           │
│                                                                     │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**The root cause:**

1. User message is saved instantly to `chat.jsonl`
2. Assistant response is only saved when `usage_update` arrives (end of stream)
3. On refresh, the server sends only what's in `chat.jsonl` (just user message)
4. Frontend's `loadChatHistory` sees `lastRole = 'user'` → no editable bubble
5. User can't type until `usage_update` arrives and `endMessageStream()` runs

**Why you see the "Agent" box disappear:**

The `loadChatHistory` does `chatContainer.innerHTML = ''` - it clears everything and rebuilds. The old tab's streaming state is wiped.

**The streaming is real-time from the agent to the NEW tab via WebSocket**, which is why you see chunks arriving - but the UI was cleared during the history load.

---

**Two possible fixes:**

1. **Server-side (cleaner)**: When client requests history, also send current streaming state if agent is actively working
2. **Client-side (simpler)**: After loading history with `lastRole = 'user'`, check if there's an active streaming session and show a temporary "waiting" state

Which direction feels right to you? Or want me to dig deeper into any part of this flow?

New flow:

   - `streaming_state` with current buffered thought and message chunks

// Track streaming message and thought buffers per session
const streamingBuffers = new Map<string, string>();
const thoughtBuffers = new Map<string, string>();
```

These are the in-memory buffers that accumulate chunks. When a client reconnects, we could check these and send the current state.

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                     SERVER-SIDE FIX DESIGN                          │

│                                                                     │
│  1. Load persisted history from chat.jsonl                        │

│                                                                     │

│  - On 'streaming_state':                                           │
│    - Recreate thought section with buffered thought                 │
│    - Recreate message content with buffered response                │
│    - Set currentMessageElement, currentThoughtElement              │
│    - Set flag: "awaiting_completion"                               │
│  - On 'usage_update' with awaiting_completion:                      │

└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

The approach involves sending buffered content to reconnecting clients so they can restore the streaming state. On the server, when a client reconnects, I'll check the streaming buffers and send any accumulated content along with the history. The client will then rebuild the message sections from this buffered state and set a flag to track that it's waiting for the streaming to complete. ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                     SERVER-SIDE FIX                                  │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                     │
│  In index.tsx, chat WebSocket open handler (line 195-214):          │
│                                                                     │
│  CURRENT:                                                           │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐        │
│  │ 1. Load history from chat.jsonl                        │        │
│  │ 2. Send { type: 'history', messages }                  │        │
│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘        │
│                                                                     │
│  NEW:                                                               │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐        │
│  │ 1. Load history from chat.jsonl                        │        │
│  │ 2. Send { type: 'history', messages }                  │        │
│  │                                                         │        │
│  │ 3. Check if session has active streaming:              │        │
│  │    - thoughtBuffers.has(sessionId)?                   │        │
│  │    - streamingBuffers.has(sessionId)?                 │        │
│  │                                                         │        │
│  │ 4. If active, send:                                    │        │
│  │    {                                                    │        │
│  │      type: 'streaming_state',                          │        │
│  │      thoughtContent: thoughtBuffers.get(sessionId),   │        │
│  │      messageContent: streamingBuffers.get(sessionId)  │        │
│  │    }                                                    │        │
│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘        │
│                                                                     │
│  In chat.js (frontend):                                            │
│                                                                     │
│  CURRENT (loadChatHistory):                                        │
│  - Clear container, rebuild from history                           │
│  - If lastRole !== 'user', create bubble                          │
│                                                                     │
│  NEW (handle streaming_state message):                             │
│  - Recreate thought section with buffered content                 │
│  - Recreate message with buffered response                        │
│  - Set streaming state variables                                  │
│  - Don't create bubble (agent is still streaming)                 │
│                                                                     │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘


```

The key is checking what chunks have accumulated in the buffers since the last `usage_update` and sending that partial state to the client so it can reconstruct the message before it completes. This way the client doesn't have to wait for the full response to start rendering.
## Agent (2026-04-08 23:18:04)

Now I have a clear picture. Here's the solution design:

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                     SERVER-SIDE FIX                                  │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                     │
│  CURRENT FLOW (index.tsx lines 195-214):                            │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐        │
│  │ 1. Load history from chat.jsonl                        │        │
│  │ 2. Send { type: 'history', messages }                  │        │
│  │ 3. Done - no info about active streaming               │        │
│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘        │
│                                                                     │
│  NEW FLOW:                                                         │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐        │
│  │ 1. Load history from chat.jsonl                        │        │
│  │ 2. Send { type: 'history', messages }                  │        │
│  │                                                         │        │
│  │ 3. Check active streaming state:                        │        │
│  │    • thoughtBuffers.get(sessionId) → thought           │        │
│  │    • streamingBuffers.get(sessionId) → message         │        │
│  │                                                         │        │
│  │ 4. If either has content, send streaming_state:        │        │
│  │    { type: 'streaming_state',                          │        │
│  │      thoughtContent: "...",                             │        │
│  │      messageContent: "..."                              │        │
│  │    }                                                   │        │
│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘        │
│                                                                     │
│  FRONTEND (chat.js) ADDITION:                                       │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐        │
│  │ Add case 'streaming_state' in handleWebSocketMessage:   │        │
│  │                                                         │        │
│  │ - Set lastRole = 'assistant' (prevents bubble)         │        │
│  │ - Call startThoughtSection()                           │        │
│  │ - Append buffered thought to currentThoughtContent     │        │
│  │ - Call endThoughtSection()                              │        │
│  │ - Call appendMessageChunk(bufferedMessage)              │        │
│  │                                                         │        │
│  │ Result: UI shows agent box with content, no bubble      │        │
│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘        │
│                                                                     │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**Key insight**: The `thoughtBuffers` and `streamingBuffers` are already accumulating chunks in real-time (see lines 416-417, 468). We just need to:
1. Send their current content when a client reconnects
2. Frontend reconstructs the streaming state

**One edge case to consider**: What if the agent was mid-thought (before `thought_end`)? The buffer would have partial thought content. We should probably check if there's also a pending `thought_start` in flight... but actually the frontend handles this - `startThoughtSection()` creates the thought section, and we'll append whatever's in the buffer.

<skill_content name="openspec-new-change">
# Skill: openspec-new-change

Start a new change using the experimental artifact-driven approach.

**Input**: The user's request should include a change name (kebab-case) OR a description of what they want to build.

**Steps**

1. **If no clear input provided, ask what they want to build**

   Use the **AskUserQuestion tool** (open-ended, no preset options) to ask:
   > "What change do you want to work on? Describe what you want to build or fix."

   From their description, derive a kebab-case name (e.g., "add user authentication" → `add-user-auth`).

   **IMPORTANT**: Do NOT proceed without understanding what the user wants to build.

2. **Determine the workflow schema**

   Use the default schema (omit `--schema`) unless the user explicitly requests a different workflow.

   **Use a different schema only if the user mentions:**
   - A specific schema name → use `--schema <name>`
   - "show workflows" or "what workflows" → run `openspec schemas --json` and let them choose

   **Otherwise**: Omit `--schema` to use the default.

3. **Create the change directory**
   ```bash
   openspec new change "<name>"
   ```
   Add `--schema <name>` only if the user requested a specific workflow.
   This creates a scaffolded change at `openspec/changes/<name>/` with the selected schema.

4. **Show the artifact status**
   ```bash
   openspec status --change "<name>"
   ```
   This shows which artifacts need to be created and which are ready (dependencies satisfied).

5. **Get instructions for the first artifact**
   The first artifact depends on the schema (e.g., `proposal` for spec-driven).
   Check the status output to find the first artifact with status "ready".
   ```bash
   openspec instructions <first-artifact-id> --change "<name>"
   ```
   This outputs the template and context for creating the first artifact.

6. **STOP and wait for user direction**

**Output**

After completing the steps, summarize:
- Change name and location
- Schema/workflow being used and its artifact sequence
- Current status (0/N artifacts complete)
- The template for the first artifact
- Prompt: "Ready to create the first artifact? Just describe what this change is about and I'll draft it, or ask me to continue."

**Guardrails**
- Do NOT create any artifacts yet - just show the instructions
- Do NOT advance beyond showing the first artifact template
- If the name is invalid (not kebab-case), ask for a valid name
- If a change with that name already exists, suggest continuing that change instead
- Pass --schema if using a non-default workflow

### Tool Call [completed]: openspec/changes/fix-chat-reconnect-streaming-state/proposal.md

content: ## Why

When a user refreshes the chat page while an agent is actively responding, the browser loses the streaming state. On reconnect, the chat history only contains the user message (since the assistant response isn't persisted until `usage_update`), causing the input box to not reappear until the agent finishes and sends `usage_update`.

## What Changes

- **Server sends current streaming state on reconnect**: When a chat WebSocket reconnects, the server checks `thoughtBuffers` and `streamingBuffers` for any in-progress content and sends it to the client
- **Frontend reconstructs streaming state**: Client receives and displays the buffered content, showing the agent box with accumulated thought/message content

## Capabilities

### New Capabilities
- `chat-streaming-state`: Capability for preserving and reconstructing in-progress streaming state on page refresh/reconnect

### Modified Capabilities
<!-- No spec-level behavior changes - this is a bug fix within existing streaming behavior -->

## Impact

- **Backend**: `index.tsx` - Modified `chat` WebSocket open handler to send streaming state
- **Frontend**: `chat.js` - New `streaming_state` message handler to reconstruct UI state

On reconnect, the chat history only contains the user message (since the assistant response isn't persisted until `usage_update`), causing the input box to not reappear until the agent finishes and sends `usage_update`.
+
+## What Changes
+
+- **Server sends current streaming state on reconnect**: When a chat WebSocket reconnects, the server checks `thoughtBuffers` and `streamingBuffers` for any in-progress content and sends it to the client
+- **Frontend reconstructs streaming state**: Client receives and displays the buffered content, showing the agent box with accumulated thought/message content
+
+## Capabilities

+
+### Modified Capabilities

+

### Tool Call [completed]: openspec/changes/fix-chat-reconnect-streaming-state/design.md

**Tool:** edit
**Timestamp:** 2026-04-08 23:19:43
**Parameters:**
content: ## Context

The chat system uses WebSocket for real-time streaming of agent responses. When an agent is actively responding:

1. `thought_start` → `thought_chunk` × N → `thought_end`
2. `message_chunk` × N
3. `usage_update` (final)

The server accumulates chunks in `thoughtBuffers` and `streamingBuffers` Maps (per session). Assistant messages are only persisted to `chat.jsonl` when `usage_update` arrives.

**Current reconnect behavior** (index.tsx lines 195-214):
- Load history from `chat.jsonl` → sends incomplete history (missing assistant response)
- Client sees `lastRole = 'user'` → no input box shown
- User cannot type until streaming completes and `usage_update` arrives

## Goals / Non-Goals

**Goals:**
- Reconstruct active streaming state on page refresh/reconnect
- Show agent box with accumulated thought and message content
- Restore user experience seamlessly

**Non-Goals:**
- Handle multiple simultaneous clients (existing limitation)
- Persist partial streaming state to disk
- Change the chunk accumulation logic

## Decisions

**1. Send streaming state alongside history on reconnect**

When a chat WebSocket connects, after sending history, check buffers and send current state:

```
// Pseudocode
const thoughtContent = thoughtBuffers.get(sessionId)
const messageContent = streamingBuffers.get(sessionId)

if (thoughtContent || messageContent) {
  ws.send(JSON.stringify({
    type: 'streaming_state',
    thoughtContent,
    messageContent,
  }))
}
```

**Why not a separate reconnect message?** Simpler - one round trip instead of two. The streaming state is only relevant immediately after connect.

**2. Frontend reconstructs state by appending buffered content**

The frontend already has `startThoughtSection()`, `appendThoughtChunk()`, `endThoughtSection()`, and `appendMessageChunk()`. We compose them:

1. Set internal flag to prevent editable bubble creation
2. Call `startThoughtSection()` (creates agent box with thought section)
3. Append buffered thought content
4. Call `endThoughtSection()`
5. Append buffered message content
6. Real-time chunks continue as normal

**Why not send separate `thought_start`/`thought_end` events?** The chunks alone are sufficient - the frontend's `startThoughtSection()` creates the thought section structure, and we just append the accumulated content.

## Risks / Trade-offs

| Risk | Mitigation |
|------|------------|
| Agent completes before buffer is sent | `usage_update` will clear buffers and send final state - client handles both |
| Multiple clients with different streaming states | Acceptable limitation - each client reconstructs independently |
| Race between reconnect and buffer clear | `usage_update` arrives after buffer content is already sent on reconnect; client handles duplicate chunks gracefully |

filePath: /home/bit4bit/src/mimo/openspec/changes/fix-chat-reconnect-streaming-state/design.md

````

╭─────────╮
│ changes │
╰─────────╯

@@ -0,0 +1,69 @@
+## Context
+
+The chat system uses WebSocket for real-time streaming of agent responses. When an agent is actively responding:
+
+1. `thought_start` → `thought_chunk` × N → `thought_end`
+2. `message_chunk` × N
+3. `usage_update` (final)
+
+The server accumulates chunks in `thoughtBuffers` and `streamingBuffers` Maps (per session). Assistant messages are only persisted to `chat.jsonl` when `usage_update` arrives.
+
+**Current reconnect behavior** (index.tsx lines 195-214):
+- Load history from `chat.jsonl` → sends incomplete history (missing assistant response)
+- Client sees `lastRole = 'user'` → no input box shown
+- User cannot type until streaming completes and `usage_update` arrives
+
+## Goals / Non-Goals
+
+**Goals:**
+- Reconstruct active streaming state on page refresh/reconnect
+- Show agent box with accumulated thought and message content
+- Restore user experience seamlessly
+
+**Non-Goals:**
+- Handle multiple simultaneous clients (existing limitation)
+- Persist partial streaming state to disk
+- Change the chunk accumulation logic
+
+## Decisions
+
+**1. Send streaming state alongside history on reconnect**
+
+When a chat WebSocket connects, after sending history, check buffers and send current state:
+
+```
+// Pseudocode
+const thoughtContent = thoughtBuffers.get(sessionId)
+const messageContent = streamingBuffers.get(sessionId)
+
+if (thoughtContent || messageContent) {
+  ws.send(JSON.stringify({
+    type: 'streaming_state',
+    thoughtContent,
+    messageContent,
+  }))
+}
+```
+
+**Why not a separate reconnect message?** Simpler - one round trip instead of two. The streaming state is only relevant immediately after connect.
+
+**2. Frontend reconstructs state by appending buffered content**
+
+The frontend already has `startThoughtSection()`, `appendThoughtChunk()`, `endThoughtSection()`, and `appendMessageChunk()`. We compose them:
+
+1. Set internal flag to prevent editable bubble creation
+2. Call `startThoughtSection()` (creates agent box with thought section)
+3. Append buffered thought content
+4. Call `endThoughtSection()`
+5. Append buffered message content
+6. Real-time chunks continue as normal
+
+**Why not send separate `thought_start`/`thought_end` events?** The chunks alone are sufficient - the frontend's `startThoughtSection()` creates the thought section structure, and we just append the accumulated content.
+
+## Risks / Trade-offs
+
+| Risk | Mitigation |
+|------|------------|
+| Agent completes before buffer is sent | `usage_update` will clear buffers and send final state - client handles both |
+| Multiple clients with different streaming states | Acceptable limitation - each client reconstructs independently |
+| Race between reconnect and buffer clear | `usage_update` arrives after buffer content is already sent on reconnect; client handles duplicate chunks gracefully |
````

**Parameters:**
content: ## chat-streaming-state

### Overview

When a chat client reconnects during an active streaming session, the server sends the current accumulated streaming state so the client can reconstruct the UI.

### WebSocket Messages

#### Server → Client

##### streaming_state

Sent immediately after `history` when reconnecting during active streaming.

```json
{
  "type": "streaming_state",
  "thoughtContent": "string (accumulated thought chunks)",
  "messageContent": "string (accumulated message chunks)",
  "timestamp": "ISO8601"
}
```

**Behavior:**
- `thoughtContent` may be empty string if no thought chunks received yet
- `messageContent` may be empty string if no message chunks received yet
- Client reconstructs streaming UI from these values

### Requirements

1. Server MUST send `streaming_state` after `history` when reconnecting during active streaming
2. Server MUST include current `thoughtContent` from `thoughtBuffers`
3. Server MUST include current `messageContent` from `streamingBuffers`
4. Client MUST display accumulated content in proper structure (thought section + message)
5. Client MUST NOT show editable bubble until `usage_update` arrives

filePath: /home/bit4bit/src/mimo/openspec/changes/fix-chat-reconnect-streaming-state/specs/chat-streaming-state.md

````

+### Overview

+
+### WebSocket Messages

+  "type": "streaming_state",

+  "messageContent": "string (accumulated message chunks)",

+}

+
+**Behavior:**

+
+### Requirements

+1. Server MUST send `streaming_state` after `history` when reconnecting during active streaming
+2. Server MUST include current `thoughtContent` from `thoughtBuffers`
+3. Server MUST include current `messageContent` from `streamingBuffers`

<skill_content name="openspec-apply-change">
# Skill: openspec-apply-change

Implement tasks from an OpenSpec change.

**Input**: Optionally specify a change name. If omitted, check if it can be inferred from conversation context. If vague or ambiguous you MUST prompt for available changes.

**Steps**

1. **Select the change**

   If a name is provided, use it. Otherwise:
   - Infer from conversation context if the user mentioned a change
   - Auto-select if only one active change exists
   - If ambiguous, run `openspec list --json` to get available changes and use the **AskUserQuestion tool** to let the user select

   Always announce: "Using change: <name>" and how to override (e.g., `/opsx:apply <other>`).

2. **Check status to understand the schema**
   ```bash
   openspec status --change "<name>" --json
   ```
   Parse the JSON to understand:
   - `schemaName`: The workflow being used (e.g., "spec-driven")
   - Which artifact contains the tasks (typically "tasks" for spec-driven, check status for others)

3. **Get apply instructions**

   ```bash
   openspec instructions apply --change "<name>" --json
   ```

   This returns:
   - Context file paths (varies by schema - could be proposal/specs/design/tasks or spec/tests/implementation/docs)
   - Progress (total, complete, remaining)
   - Task list with status
   - Dynamic instruction based on current state

   **Handle states:**
   - If `state: "blocked"` (missing artifacts): show message, suggest using openspec-continue-change
   - If `state: "all_done"`: congratulate, suggest archive
   - Otherwise: proceed to implementation

4. **Read context files**

   Read the files listed in `contextFiles` from the apply instructions output.
   The files depend on the schema being used:
   - **spec-driven**: proposal, specs, design, tasks
   - Other schemas: follow the contextFiles from CLI output

5. **Show current progress**

   Display:
   - Schema being used
   - Progress: "N/M tasks complete"
   - Remaining tasks overview
   - Dynamic instruction from CLI

6. **Implement tasks (loop until done or blocked)**

   For each pending task:
   - Show which task is being worked on
   - Make the code changes required
   - Keep changes minimal and focused
   - Mark task complete in the tasks file: `- [ ]` → `- [x]`
   - Continue to next task

   **Pause if:**
   - Task is unclear → ask for clarification
   - Implementation reveals a design issue → suggest updating artifacts
   - Error or blocker encountered → report and wait for guidance
   - User interrupts

7. **On completion or pause, show status**

   Display:
   - Tasks completed this session
   - Overall progress: "N/M tasks complete"
   - If all done: suggest archive
   - If paused: explain why and wait for guidance

**Output During Implementation**

```
## Implementing: <change-name> (schema: <schema-name>)

Working on task 3/7: <task description>
[...implementation happening...]
✓ Task complete

Working on task 4/7: <task description>
[...implementation happening...]
✓ Task complete
```

**Output On Completion**

```
## Implementation Complete

**Change:** <change-name>
**Schema:** <schema-name>
**Progress:** 7/7 tasks complete ✓

### Completed This Session
- [x] Task 1
- [x] Task 2
...

All tasks complete! Ready to archive this change.
```

**Output On Pause (Issue Encountered)**

```
## Implementation Paused

**Change:** <change-name>
**Schema:** <schema-name>
**Progress:** 4/7 tasks complete

### Issue Encountered
<description of the issue>

**Options:**
1. <option 1>
2. <option 2>
3. Other approach

What would you like to do?
```

**Guardrails**
- Keep going through tasks until done or blocked
- Always read context files before starting (from the apply instructions output)
- If task is ambiguous, pause and ask before implementing
- If implementation reveals issues, pause and suggest artifact updates
- Keep code changes minimal and scoped to each task
- Update task checkbox immediately after completing each task
- Pause on errors, blockers, or unclear requirements - don't guess
- Use contextFiles from CLI output, don't assume specific file names

**Fluid Workflow Integration**

This skill supports the "actions on a change" model:

- **Can be invoked anytime**: Before all artifacts are done (if tasks exist), after partial implementation, interleaved with other actions
- **Allows artifact updates**: If implementation reveals design issues, suggest updating artifacts - not phase-locked, work fluidly

Base directory for this skill: file:///home/bit4bit/src/mimo/.opencode/skills/openspec-apply-change
Relative paths in this skill (e.g., scripts/, reference/) are relative to this base directory.
Note: file list is sampled.

<skill_files>

</skill_files>
</skill_content>
````
